scenarios.opening_query: My grandmother's recipes became my bakery's signature line and a chain is now selling knockoffs.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Recipes themselves aren't protectable — but the chain got them from my former production manager who signed an NDA; the trade-secret/contract case against him is the real path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Recipes aren't protectable — but the chain got them from my ex-production manager, who signed an NDA. That contract case is the path.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an NDA/trade-secret theory replaces unavailable IP protection
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're also using my trademarked product names and my packaging art pixel-for-pixel — registered marks, registered copyrights.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They copied my registered product names and packaging art pixel-for-pixel.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recipe-unprotectability with NDA rescue; sophisticated but realistic.

scenarios.opening_query: A gaming company used artwork almost identical to my portfolio piece in their new release.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I interviewed there last year and did a paid 'art test' — their own files hold my piece, and the art-test agreement I signed decides who owns what.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I did a paid 'art test' for them last year — they have my piece on file, and that agreement decides ownership.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a signed art-test agreement governs
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I've never had any contact with them — pure copying from my public portfolio, registered last year.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No contact ever — pure copying from my registered public portfolio.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone is selling 3D-printed copies of the miniatures I sculpt and sell online.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: They ripped my actual design files — my store's file server was breached and my print files are circulating; the intrusion and file distribution is the case beyond any copying.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They ripped my actual files — my store's server was breached and my print files are circulating. The intrusion is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a data breach underlies the copying
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No breach — they hand-modeled imitations of my published designs; classic infringement analysis.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No breach — they hand-modeled imitations of my published designs.

scenarios.opening_query: My co-founder left and claims he owns half the app we built because he 'named it.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: He quietly filed a trademark application for the app name in HIS own name last month — opposing that filing has its own deadline and is the immediate fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed a trademark application for the name in his own name — opposing it has a deadline, and that's the immediate fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a pending adverse trademark filing needs opposition
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No filings anywhere — it's the founders' equity split under our sloppy operating agreement.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filings — it's the equity split under our sloppy operating agreement.

scenarios.opening_query: A national brand sent my Etsy shop a letter demanding I destroy all my inventory.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: My products are genuine items I buy retail and resell — first-sale doctrine covers me, and their letter's threats against lawful resale look like the abusive practice to counter.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My products are genuine retail items I resell — first-sale covers me, and their threats against lawful resale are the abuse to counter.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether genuine-goods resale defeats the infringement theory
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Honestly, I do print their logo on my blanks — I thought parody covered me, and I need a real infringement assessment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Honestly I print their logo on blanks — I thought parody covered me. I need a real assessment.

scenarios.opening_query: The university claims it owns the diagnostic tool I invented in my own garage on weekends.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only) || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Everything turns on my faculty employment agreement's invention-assignment clause and whether weekend work using no university resources falls inside it — an employment-contract interpretation before any patent step.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It all turns on my faculty contract's invention-assignment clause and whether garage weekends count — employment-contract interpretation first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an employment IP-assignment clause is the gateway question
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The university released its claim in writing — the patent application itself, before my publication date, is the need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They released their claim in writing — now it's the patent filing before my publication date.
scenarios.vetting_note: Assignment-clause gateway vs cleared filing path.
